seoTitle: Gorrie vs Gosnells — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gorrie and Gosnells. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gorrie and Gosnells.
heading: Gorrie vs Gosnells — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gorrie, Western Australia and Gosnells, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gorrie has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Gosnells (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gorrie or Gosnells safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gorrie has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Gosnells scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gorrie scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Gosnells scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gorrie or Gosnells?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gorrie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
